_about: Conventional-Commits grammar manifest for Mycelium. The single declarative source for mapping a commit/PR-title `type(scope): subject` to the repo's type:* / area:* labels (and for inferring a milestone). Consumed by gh-issues-sync.py --prs. House rule: never invent (G2) — an unmapped type or a scope that is not an area label is FLAGGED, never guessed. The area:* set itself lives in labels.json (DRY); this file only adds the optional, declared scope aliases.
title_grammar: type(scope)!: subject  — Conventional Commits 1.0.0. scope is optional; a trailing ! on the type (or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer) marks a breaking change. The PR-title path falls back to the PR's commit messages when the title itself does not parse.

type_to_label_repo_ext._about: Repo-specific 'friends' of Conventional Commits the maintainer already uses in titles (e.g. `spec(adr-011): ...`). Append-only, ratified extensions — merged over type_to_label by the engine. Add a type here (a decision) instead of letting it FLAG.

scope_to_area._policy: A scope maps to area:<scope> ONLY when <scope> is exactly an area:* label in labels.json; otherwise it is FLAGGED, never invented (G2). To turn a recurring FLAG into a mapping, add a declared alias below — each alias is a ratified decision, not a guess. The VALUE is the BARE area name; the engine prepends `area:` (derive_pr_labels), so write `language`, NOT `area:language`. Doc-reference scopes (rfc-*/adr-*/dn-*), multi-scope comma titles, and task-id scopes (m-*) are deliberately left UNMAPPED — they stay FLAGGED rather than guessed.

milestone_inference._policy: For a PR, scan the title + body + commit messages for task-ids; resolve each via issues.yaml to its milestone. Set the milestone ONLY when every resolved id agrees on a single milestone (unambiguous); otherwise FLAG and set nothing (never invent a milestone).

scope_to_milestone._policy: Declared scope→milestone fallback, consulted ONLY when task-id inference yields nothing (G2: task-ids always win). Map a PR's conventional-commit scope(s) to a milestone title ONLY when the mapping is unambiguous: if multiple scopes resolve to DIFFERENT milestones, FLAG and set nothing. Scopes not in the aliases map are FLAGGED for the maintainer and left unset — never guessed. Phase-ambiguous code-phase scopes (mlir, l1, grammar, surface, jit, llvm, runtime, vsa, numerics, dense, bitnet, select, swap, std, stdlib, spec, kc2, and earlier-phase planning scopes) are deliberately left UNMAPPED so they remain flagged rather than wrongly assigned.

breaking_change._policy: A breaking marker (type! or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer) is reported in the never-silent output. There is no breaking:* label in labels.json today, so it is FLAGGED for the maintainer rather than mapped to an invented label.
